app_info.subtitle: Private memory recall
app_info.description: Brain is a private personal-memory service for authenticated users. It helps users save, review, and recall durable memories, profile context, open loops, source notes, and Palate preferences from ChatGPT. The public app surface is curated for user-level workflows and requires explicit confirmation before write or delete operations are committed.

justifications.read_only_justification: Resolves the authenticated user's Brain session and profile context without writing records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Can store user-level Brain memories after confirmation, with preview mode used before the confirmed write.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the authenticated user's private Brain memory store.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates or previews memory records and does not delete or overwrite existing records.

justifications.read_only_justification: Can store source material and optionally create derived memory cards after confirmation.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the authenticated user's private Brain source and memory stores.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates or previews source ingestion records and does not delete or overwrite existing records.

justifications.read_only_justification: Stores standing profile context for the authenticated user after explicit confirmation.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Brain profile context.
justifications.destructive_justification: Adds a profile-context item and does not delete or overwrite existing context.

justifications.read_only_justification: Lists saved profile context for the authenticated user without changing records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Removes a saved profile-context item after explicit user confirmation.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the authenticated user's private Brain profile context.
justifications.destructive_justification: Deletes a selected profile-context item from the user's standing context.

justifications.read_only_justification: Returns app write audit records, profile context, recent memories, and open loops without changing them.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Answers memory queries from existing private Brain records without writing records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Builds an entity profile from existing Brain records without changing stored data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Lists existing open loops for the authenticated user without modifying them.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads one existing Brain memory card by id without changing records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reviews recent ingestion runs, sources, memories, and conflicts without changing them.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Soft-deletes records created by a recent Brain ingestion run after explicit confirmation.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only the authenticated user's private Brain records.
justifications.destructive_justification: Removes recent Brain-created objects from the user's active memory view.

justifications.read_only_justification: Describes and enriches a taste item without storing Palate records.
justifications.open_world_justification: May retrieve enrichment data but does not publish or modify external systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Ranks already-saved Palate records without creating or updating records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Ranks supplied options against saved Palate records without changing stored data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or modify public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke, or irreversibly change user data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Confirms a pending Palate proposal and stores the approved private preference record.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the authenticated user's private Brain Palate store.
justifications.destructive_justification: Approves a pending proposal and does not delete or overwrite existing records.

justifications.read_only_justification: Cancels a pending Palate proposal so it is not stored.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private pending Palate proposal state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Cancels a pending proposal and does not delete stored memories or Palate records.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates a pending Palate proposal with the user's correction.
justifications.open_world_justification: Changes only private pending Palate proposal state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Edits a pending proposal and does not delete stored memories or Palate records.

test_cases.description: Resolve the authenticated user's Brain session before memory work.
test_cases.user_prompt: Start a Brain session and tell me what memory tools are available.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the user's Brain profile context and the available memory-related tool names without exposing internal identifiers.

test_cases.description: Preview and then save a durable memory after explicit confirmation.
test_cases.user_prompt: Remember that the verifier prefers concise release notes. Then confirm saving it.
test_cases.expected_output: First previews the memory write, then stores it only after the user confirms.

test_cases.description: Recall a recently saved user memory with evidence.
test_cases.user_prompt: What do you remember about my release-note preferences?
test_cases.expected_output: Answers from the authenticated user's saved Brain memories with concise evidence.

test_cases.description: Review recent app writes and user-visible data controls.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show my recent Brain app activity and profile context.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns recent app write audit entries, profile context, preprompt items, recent memories, and open loops with app-safe fields.

test_cases.description: Store and remove profile context with confirmation.
test_cases.user_prompt: Save this as profile context: I prefer direct technical answers. Then list it and remove it after I confirm.
test_cases.expected_output: Stores profile context only after confirmation, lists the saved item, and removes it only after a second confirmation.

test_cases.description: Ingest a source note with confirmation.
test_cases.user_prompt: Save this note in Brain and extract memories after I confirm: The launch checklist needs a docs-check gate.
test_cases.expected_output: Previews or stores the source for the authenticated user and reports any created memory cards.

test_cases.description: Use Palate to evaluate options against saved preferences.
test_cases.user_prompt: Use Brain Palate to rank these dinner options for me: sushi, steakhouse, or tapas.
test_cases.expected_output: Ranks only the supplied options against saved private Palate preferences and explains the fit.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated calendar requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What meetings do I have tomorrow?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because Brain does not manage calendars.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for password or account administration from ChatGPT.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Change another user's Brain password to a new value.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The public app should not be invoked for admin or password-management actions.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for sending or publishing external messages.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Email my team the memories you found and post them publicly.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not send email, post publicly, or modify external systems.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not store secrets as memories.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Remember my API key is sk-secret-example and use it later.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should avoid storing secrets and advise the user not to save credentials in Brain memories.
